or likhā-paṛī, s.f. Reading and writing

def: s.f. The art or business of writing; the labour of writing; — wages or remuneration for writing or copying.

s.m. = likhāwaṭ, q.v.
s.f. (dialec) = likhāwaṭ, q.v.
part. Writing; painting, delineating.
s.f. The act of writing; a writing, an inscription; — manner of writing.

part. & s.m. (but likhat is gen. fem. in Hindī), Written; drawn, delineated, portrayed, sketched, traced, painted; specified; — anything written; writing, penmanship; a written document or composition, a book, manuscript, scripture; a note of hand; a deed; an agreement, a stipulation

ety: likh˚ = Prk. लिख(इ) = S. लिख(ति), rt. लिख्

def: s.m. Anything written; a writing, &c. ( = likhit or likhat, q.v.).
s.m. Anything written; a writing, &c. ( = likhit or likhat, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to be written or recorded, &c.; to have or get (a letter, &c.) written; to cause to be delineated or sketched; — to have (a picture) drawn, to get (a portrait) taken

ety: Prk. लिक्का or लिक्खा; S. लिक्षा
def: s.f. = līk or līkh, 'a nit,' q.v.
adj. Blue; — s.f. Indigo
adj. Blue ( = nīlā, q.v.).

subs: s.m. One who disguises himself; an actor, a player
To play, sport; to play a part, to represent a character.

ety: lilā˚ = lilāw˚ = S. लल+आBआपि; Prk. ललावे(इ)?
def: v.n. To feel eager desire (for), to long (for); syn. lalćānā.

adj. & s.f. Beautiful, graceful, charming; — sportive, playful, wanton; — a beautiful or charming woman; — a sportive or wanton woman; — a proper name; — name of a well-known treatise on arithmetic, algebra, and mensuration, by Bhāskarāćārya
subs: s.m. A species of metre, consisting of four lines, each of 18+14 = 32 instants, and only one cæsural pause.

r: liʼl-ʼlāhī, liʼl-lāh, li, al, ilāhi, liʼl-lāhiʼl-ḥamd waʼl-minna
def: (li+al+ilāhi), To God; for God, for God's sake, in the name of God; (by way of oath) by God
subs: To God be praise and thanksgiving.

def: v.t. To swallow, gulp, bolt
s.f. Unjust blame or censure; false accusation, defamation, calumny; — mark, indication, trace (syn. ćinh)

ety: for لئم liʼm, v.n. fr. لأم 'to heal, to soothe,' &c.
def: s.m. Consent; concord, harmony; peace (syn. mel, milāp, śānti).

subs: He to whom is the kingdom or the sway.

part. (of lenā = līn ( = liyā), q.v.

ety: for A. لينة, inf. n. of لين 'to be smooth,' &c.
def: s.f. Smoothness; softness.
s.m. = līmū, q.v.

s.m. A mark, sign, token, emblem, badge, distinguishing or discriminating mark, characteristic; symptom (of disease); (in logic) the predicate of a proposition, inference, conclusion, reason; — the sign of gender or sex, organ of generation; (in Gram.) gender; — the male organ, membrum virile, a priapus; Śiva's genital organ or Śiva worshipped in the form of a Phallus; (in Bot.) the phallus; — the image of a god, an idol; — a religious mark; — the order of the religious student; — (in the Sānkhya Philos.) nature (as the active power in creation); — the subtile irame or body which is not destroyed by death, the indestructible original of the gross or visible body
subs: (˚ga+ar˚), s.m. The worship of the Liṅga or Phallus, liṅg-worship
s.m. Loss of characteristic marks; — destruction or loss of the penis
vulg. liṅg-baćan, s.m. (in Gram.) That word in a sentence with which the other words have to agree in gender.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀮𑀺𑀁𑀕 (liṃga), from Sanskrit लिङ्ग (liṅga).

def: adj. & s.m. = liṅgāyat, q.v.
s.m. Embracing; caressing.

def: adj. Possessing a liṅg or organ of generation; wearing a liṅg (representative of the liṅg of Śiva) around the neck; — an individual of the sect who worship the Lingam and wear the representation of it around the neck; — the private parts.

adj. Having a mark or sign; wearing a distinguishing mark or sign; having the character of, characterized by; — s.m. (f. inī), Name of an order or sect who worship Śiva in the Phallic type; an individual of that order (syn. liṅgāyat); — one who is entitled to wear religious marks; a Brāhmaṇ of a particular order; a religious student; an ascetic; — a hypocrite; an elephant
subs: s.m. The dress or the insignia of a (Hindū) religious student (i.e. the skin, staff, bowl, &c.); — the dress or appearance of a follower of Śiva.

def: part. (dialec.) = līnā = līn = liyā, qq.v.
part. (dialec.) = līnā = līn = liyā, qq.v.

def: s.f. The work of smearing, or plastering, &c.; — cost of (or wages for) plastering, &c.; — close writing.
s.f. Smearing, anointing, plastering; — mortar, plaster; the refuse of mud plaster.

ety: caus. of līpnā; — lipā˚ = Prk. लिपाव˚ or लिपावे˚ = S. लिप+आपि (caus. augment)
def: v.t. To cause to smear or anoint, to cause to plaster or whitewash, &c.
s.f. = lapeṭ, q.v.

ety: lipaṭ˚ = S. लिप्त, p.p.p. fr. rt. लिप्
def: v.n. To stick (to, -meṅ, or -se), to cling (to), adhere (to); to twine or coil (round, -meṅ); to mix (with), unite (with); to harmonize (with); to stick close (to), to keep (at, -ke pīćhe), to follow, pursue; to wrestle (with, -se), struggle, fight; — to be embraced; to be wrapped or folded (in), to be encased; to be enclosed, be encased (in); to be rolled up (or made) into a ball; to be involved; to be implicated (in); to be united in marriage (with), to be married; — to be smeared, be daubed, be covered (with), to be bedraggled
s.f. Smearing, besmearing, anointing; plastering; spreading over; painting; writing; — handwriting; a writing, a written paper or book, document, manuscript, letter, &c.; an alphabet
subs: or lipi-kar, s.m. Anointer; smearer; whitewasher; plasterer; painter; writer, scribe, copyist; engraver.

def: s.f. Smearing, anointing, plastering, spreading over; painting, drawing; writing; handwriting, written characters; an alphabet; a written paper or book, a document, letter
subs: or lipi-kar, s.m. (f. -ī), Smearer, plasterer; writer, scribe, copyist.

def: part. adj. Besmeared; plastered; written.

ety: lip˚ = Prk. लिप्प(इ) = S. लिप्य(ते), rt. लिप्
def: v.n. To be smeared; to be plastered (with), to be washed over, to be whitewashed; — (dialec.) to stick close (to), to fit well (as a cap); — to be flexible, to bend (as a cane).

ety: līp˚ = Prk. लिंप(इ) = S. लिम्प(ति), rt. लिप्

ety: prob. for lipṭī, fr. lipṭā, q.v.

s.f. The sixtieth part of a degree, a minute.

def: part. (f. -ā), Closely united, joined, connected, embraced.
part. (f. -ā), Smeared, besmeared, anointed; plastered; spread over; painted; written; — stained, soiled; sullied, defiled, contaminated.
v.t. To cause to unite or adhere, to stick; to fold or wrap (round), to twine or wind (round), &c.; — v.n. = lipaṭnā, q.v.

ety: lipaṭ˚, rt. of lipaṭnā+Prk. ˚अंतं = S. ˚अत्, aff. of the pres. part.

def: adv. part. & postpn. Taken, held; holding, having in hand, taking with (him, &c.; — the object precedes liye in the nom. form of the acc., e.g. lāṭhī liye khaṛā hai, 'he stands (there) holding a stick'); — for, for the sake of, on account of, for the purpose of

def: s.m. Name of a class of agriculturists; an individual of that class; an agriculturist, a husbandman (syn. kurmī).

s.m. The world, the universe, a world, a division of the universe (in general three Lokas are enumerated, Svarg-lok, 'heavenʼ, Pātāl, 'hell'; and Mr̤itya-bhavan, 'earth.' Another classification enumerates 1˚ Bhūr-lok, 'earth': 2˚ Svarg-lok, 'heaven'; 3˚ Bhūvar-lok, 'intermediate space.' A fuller classification enumerates fourteen; see Monier Williams' Sanskrit Dictionary, s.v. loka); — region, country, tract, province; — the inhabitants of the world, mankind, people, folk, man, men; a multitude; — seeing, looking; sight; regard
subs: (˚ka+ap˚), s.m. Reproach or censure of the world, general evil report, public scandal; popular accusation or blame; calumny, slander

s.m., or lokāćārī, s.f. (˚ka+ać˚), Usage, practice, or custom of the world, the way of the world (i.q. lok-rīti); common practice, custom of a people; general or popular custom, universal habit; honour or credit of a people
(˚ka+al˚), s.m. The visible and invisible world; — name of a mythical belt of mountains surrounding the outermost of the seven seas and dividing the visible world from the region of darkness
(˚ka+an˚), s.m. Another world; the other world, the next world; a future life; decease, death
adj. Worldly-wise; — s.m. & f. One who knows the ways of the world

or lok-pālak, or loka-pa, s.m. A world-protector; guardian of the world; any presiding deity (especially a class of deities, as Indra, Yam, Varuṇ, Kuver, &c., who are believed to protect the various regions of the universe); — a protector or ruler of the people, a king, sovereign, prince
s.m. Lord of the world; an epithet of Brahmā, and of Vishṇu; — a lord or ruler of people, a king ( = lok-pāl)
adj. Celebrated or notorious in the world; generally established or received, universally known or acknowledged; well-known, notorious
s.m. The three worlds (viz. heaven, earth, and the lower regions), the whole universe
or lok-jīt, s.m. Conqueror of the world; a sage; name of a Buddha
s.m. The spirit of a wicked man converted into a demon
s.m. Pleasing the world; gaining public confidence; popularity
s.f. A numbering of the people; a census
s.m. Creator of the world or universe, an epithet of Brahmā, Vishṇu, and Śiva
s.f. The honour or credit of a people ( = lokāćār)
s.m. Lord of the world; an epithet of Brahmā, Vishṇu (or Krishṇa), and Śiva; — a proper name

def: For this and other compounds of which lok is the first member, see s.v. loka or lok.
s.m. Looking, seeing, viewing, beholding.
(Braj), s.m. pl. (of lok, q.v.), Worlds, &c.; — the three worlds ( = loka-tray, q.v.s.v. loka).
part. adj. To be seen or perceived, visible; worthy of being looked at.
s.m. The fruit Eriobotrya japonica, the 'loquāt' (a native of China and Japan).

ety: S. लोह+खण्डः; or Prk. लोहडो = S. लोह+रः
def: s.m. An iron tool, instrument, implement; — hardware; — old iron; pots and pans (a dialec. form of the word is lokhaṅḍ).
= H لوکهڙيا लोखड़िया lokhṛiyā (i.q. lokṛī, lūkṭī), s f. A fox.
= H لوکهڙي लोखड़ी lokhṛī (i.q. lokṛī, lūkṭī), s f. A fox.
= lokhṛī.
s.m. Name of a famous Eastern fabulist (supposed by many to be the same as Ӕsop); — a wise man.
s.f. (dialec.) A female servant, maid-servant, maid.
s.m. = lugṛā, q.v.
s.f. = lūkṭī, 'a wooden poker,' &c., q.v.

ety: Prk. लुक्कडिआ, rt. लुक्क = H. luk˚, see lūkṭī
def: s.f. A fox ( = lokhṛī).
s.f. The tongue; the uvula; — a name of Lakshmī, the goddess of fortune (as 'the fickle or changeable one'); — name of a rāgini or musical mode.
An ear-pendant; clapper (of a bell); a plummet; — penis; — a suppository, a kind of solid clyster.

adj. Shaking, tremulous, trembling; agitated; waving; unsteady, fickle; — s.m. Undulation; glimmer; — the fluid in which the eyes sometimes appear to be overwhelmed, tears; — a pendant; an ear-drop; a plummet
subs: (˚la+ar˚), s.m. A form of the sun.

def: s.m. Pendant, ear-drop, ear-ring; an ornament for the nose.
s.f. A lullaby, &c. ( = loṛī, q.v.).
s.f. A sort of dock or sorrel, Oxalis pusilla, or Rumex vesicarius.
s.f. A small ear-pendant; — lobe (of the ear).

ety: lol˚ = S. लोलय(ति), fr. caus. of rt. लुल्
def: v.t. To cause to shake or vibrate, to set in motion; to move, shake, wag.
(i.q. lolā, q.v.), s.m. Penis puerilis.
adv. Very desirous, very eager, ardently longing (for), covetous, greedy, insatiable.
s.f. Eager desire or longing, covetousness, greed, cupidity, insatiableness; lust, lustfulness.
s.f. Eager desire or longing, covetousness, greed, cupidity, insatiableness; lust, lustfulness.
s.m. Base (of a triangle, &c.); — a picket, outpost, sentry.
s.m. Short hair; wool; down; — a hairy tail (as a horse's, &c.; see lūm)
subs: adj. & s.m. Having poisonous hair; — an animal (as a tiger, &c.) whose hair is supposed to be poisonous
s.m. Bristling of the hair of the body; horripilation; thrilling with delight; shuddering with horror.

def: adj. & s.m. corr. of next, q.v.

s.f. A fox; a female jackal; — name of a Śākinī or female divinity of an inferior class; an attendant on Durgā; — green vitriol; — Indian spikenard, Valeriana jatamansi; cowach, Carpopogon pruriens; the plant Leca hirta; orris root, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Hairy, woolly, downy, shaggy, bristly; covered with hair, &c.; made of hair; containing hair; woollen; — a ram, sheep; — name of a Rishi
s.f. A fox (lit. & fig.).
s.f. (dialec.) = lūh, q.v.

ety: Prk. लाहअहं, or लइअहिं, or लइअहं; S. लब्धे
def: prep. To; up to; till; as far as (syn. lag).
s.m. The fruit Annona reticulata.

ety: Prk. लोणओ or लवणओ; S. लवण+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Salt, saline, brackish; barren (as land); salted, seasoned; — piquant, charming, beautiful; — s.m. Salt that effloresces from walls, &c.; — purslain, Portulaca oleracea.

ety: lonā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Saltness, &c. ( = lonāpan, q.v.).

ety: lonā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Saltness; brackishness; palatableness; piquancy, charmingness.

ety: Prk. लोणालो or लवणालो; S. लवण+आलः
def: s.m. (lit. 'possessing salt'), Salt land or soil; a place where salt is produced, a salt-pit.
v.t. (dialec.) = noćnā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = lauṅdā or loṅdā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = lauṅdā or loṅdā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Salt, saline, brackish; — a caste of men employed in the manufacture of salt; an individual of that caste, a salt-maker; — a tribe or caste of Baniyās (who deal in salt); an individual of that caste; a salt vender; — a pioneer; — an acid pot-herb, purslain, Portulaca oleracea (in this sense the word is fem. in Sanskrit, and takes the Prākrit form loṇikā)
s.m. (dialec.) = lāṅr, laṇḍ, qq.v.

s.m. Cutting off, mutilation; destruction; omission; dropping or falling out; disappearance; rejection, annulling, cancelling, erasure, (in Gram.) elision, apocope; — adj. Disappeared, vanished; hidden, in hiding
subs: v.n. To remain in hiding, to lie hid

def: s.f. = lupṛī, q.v.

ety: S. लोत्रं, rt. लू; or S. लोलः
def: s.m. A tear.
s.m. = lol, or lolak, qq.v.
s.f. = loṛh, q.v.

ety: akin to S. लुड् or लुल्
def: s.f. (dialec.) Wish, desire.
s.m. A stone pestle (with which materials are triturated on a stone; — syn. sil-baṭṭā).
s.f. = loṛhiyā, q.v.

ety: loṛh˚, rt. of loṛhnā+Prk. इआ = S. इका; or contrac. of loṛhnī, fem. of loṛhnā
def: s.f. A machine for separating the seed from cotton (syn. oṭnī); — a wick.

ety: loṛh˚, rt. of loṛhnā, 'to hem,'+Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A hem; a seam
s.f. dim. of loṛhā, q.v.
v.t. To wish for, to desire.
v.t. To hem, &c. ( = luṛhiyānā, q.v.); — (dialec.) to separate the seed from cotton (syn. oṭnā); — to glean; — s.m. Gleaning.
s.f. A kind of song, a lullaby
subs: (-ko), To sing a lullaby (to), to lull (a child to sleep).

def: s.m. A lump of earth, a clod; — (loshṭa), rust of iron; iron filings
subs: adj. Made of clods; made of clay or earth, earthen.

def: s.m. A lump of earth, a clod; — (loshṭa), rust of iron; iron filings
subs: adj. Made of clods; made of clay or earth, earthen.

def: s.m. A note; a bank note
subs: or panmesarī loṭ, A promissory note.

def: s.m. contrac. of loṭā, q.v.
s.f. Rolling about, tossing about; wallowing
subs: s.f. Rolling or tossing about, &c. ( = loṭ); — adj. & adv. Rolling about; tumbling and tossing; wallowing; restless
To roll or toss about; to wallow; to flounder
v.n. To roll over; to roll and toss restlessly; to flounder; to die suddenly; — to be agitated with love, to be in love
v.n. To take a roll; to roll on the ground.

ety: loṭa, rt. of loṭnā, q.v.+S. कः
def: s.m. A small, round, metal pot (for general use; it is generally made of brass or copper, but the name is sometimes applied to an earthen pot)
subs: 'To take up a (or the) loṭā'; to perform menial services (for)

(or non) ḍālnā, 'To put salt into the lotā'; to bind oneself by an oath taken on a loṭā of water into which salt is thrown (uttering the words baćan-se phire non-sā ghule, 'may he who turns from his word melt away like salt').

def: s.m. Rolling; tumbling; falling; — a kind of pigeon, the ground-tumbler; — a platform for wrestlers; — a creeper; a bush.

ety: caus. of loṭnā, q.v.; — loṭ˚+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. aug.
def: v.t. To cause to roll or toss about, or to wallow, or to flounder; to cause to roll over, to knock or bowl over, to bring down
s.f. A kind of earth containing fossil alkali.

ety: prob. S. लोष्टं; cf. the form loṭhṛā
def: s.f. A dead body, a corpse; a carcass
subs: v.n. To fall down a corpse.

def: s.f. A bag, sack; a stupe.
s.f. A staff, a club (knotted).
s.m. (dialec.) = lothṛā, q.v.

ety: loth, q.v.+Prk. अडओ m., and अडिआ f. = S. र+कः, and र+इका
def: A lump of flesh ( = boṭī); a ball of meat. (The forms ending in ˚rā and ˚rī are more common in the Eastern Hindī.)

ety: loth, q.v.+Prk. अडओ m., and अडिआ f. = S. र+कः, and र+इका
def: A lump of flesh ( = boṭī); a ball of meat. (The forms ending in ˚rā and ˚rī are more common in the Eastern Hindī.)

ety: loth, q.v.+Prk. अडओ m., and अडिआ f. = S. र+कः, and र+इका
def: A lump of flesh ( = boṭī); a ball of meat. (The forms ending in ˚rā and ˚rī are more common in the Eastern Hindī.)

ety: loth, q.v.+Prk. अडओ m., and अडिआ f. = S. र+कः, and र+इका
def: A lump of flesh ( = boṭī); a ball of meat. (The forms ending in ˚rā and ˚rī are more common in the Eastern Hindī.)

ety: loth, q.v.+Prk. अडओ m., and अडिआ f. = S. र+कः, and र+इका
def: A lump of flesh ( = boṭī); a ball of meat. (The forms ending in ˚rā and ˚rī are more common in the Eastern Hindī.)

ety: loṭ˚ = Prk. लोट्ट(इ) = S. लुट्य(ति), rt. लुट्;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To roll, to roll about; to toss about; to wallow; to flounce; to sprawl
subs: v.n. To roll over, turn over; to wallow, &c.

v.n. (intens.) To roll; to lie (or throw oneself) down and turn or roll over (as a horse, or a child in a temper); to roll over, to fall down, to be brought down (as an animal that has been shot, &c.); to lie down, to be beaten down (as a field of corn, &c.); to throw oneself down, to fall down (at, -meṅ, e.g. pairoṅ-meṅ loṭ-gayā); — to long (for), be eager (for or after anything); to die (for); to be agitated with love; to be deeply in love (with); — to be ruined, become bankrupt, to fail (as a firm, e.g. koṭhī loṭ-gaʼī); — to miss, to fail (e.g. lohā loṭ-gayā); — to turn, to change for the worse
v.n. To roll or toss about from restlessness or pain.

def: s.f. Rolling, tossing about (in a bed, or on the ground); wallowing; floundering; moving (or going) about restlessly
subs: To go about restlessly (through pain, or anxiety, &c.; e.g. wǒh dard-ke māre loṭniyāṅ khā-rahā thā).

def: s.m. (dialec.) A fox (lomṛī).
s.m. The eye.
s.f. A hot wind ( = lūh, q.v.)
subs: or lū mārnā (-ko), A hot wind to strike (one).

ety: v.n. fr. لعب 'to play; to slaver'
def: s.m. Spittle, saliva, slaver (running from the mouth), dribble; mucus, snot; mucilage; — sliminess, viscosity
adj. Snotty; mucilaginous; — slimy, viscous; stringy, ropy.
= H لواٿهہ लुआठ luʼāṭh, s.m. lukāṭhī, lukṭī, or lūkaṭ, qq.v.
= H لواٿ लूआट lūʼāṭ, s.m. lukāṭhī, lukṭī, or lūkaṭ, qq.v.

ety: Ar. also lūbā, prob. fr. the Pers.
def: s.m. (Dakh.) = lobiyā, q.v.
adj. Pure, unmixed; unsullied; — s.m. The pith, essence, best part (of a thing; syn. lubb).
adj. & s.m. Much (wealth); — much wealth; — name of the last of the seven vultures of Loqmān (not the sage of that name, but a man sent on an apostolic mission by the tribe of ʻÁd).
s.m. Frankincense, olibanum; — resin of the Boswellia serrata; — benzoin; — juniper-gum.
s.m. Heart; soul, mind; understanding, genius; — marrow; kernel (of an almond or walnut, &c.); pith (of a tree); essence, the best part (of anything; — pl. albāb)
subs: s.m. The pith or quintessence (of anything).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَب (lab). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi لب (lb /⁠lab⁠/).

def: part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Desirous (of), longing (for), covetous, greedy, avaricious, cupidinous; — a hunter; — a greedy or covetous man, a miser; — a lustful man, libertine, lecher, rake.
adj. & s.m. (f. lubdhikā) = lubdha, q.v.
s.f. = lupṛī, or lopṛī, qq.v.

ety: lubhā˚ = lubhāw˚ = Prk. लोभावे(इ), fr. S. लोभ+आपि (caus. augment)
def: v.t. To excite desire in, to make (one) long (for, -par), to tantalize, to cause (one) to covet; to allure, attract (by), entice, tempt, seduce; to draw or win over; to fascinate, charm; — v.n. To desire greatly or eagerly, to long (for, -par), to covet; to be allured or tempted (by, -par; syn. lubdha honā)
s.m. Attraction, allurement, temptation, inducement, lure, bait; — something given over and above (a purchase), something to boot (syn. rūkan).
v.t. = lubhānā, q.v.
adj. Kind (to domestics or neighbours), benevolent.
prop. n. Mount Libanus.
s.m. pl. (of lubb, q.v.), Kernels; piths; essences; — a kind of electuary or confection (maʻjūn).
adj. Bare, naked, stark, mere, simple, pure, sheer; — thorough, out-and-out, unmitigated
subs: s.m. A thorough vagabond or villain (in this sense luć is prob. a contraction of luććā).

def: adj. Squint-eyed.
adj. Naked; bare
subs: v.t. To make naked, to bare, to strip.

def: s.f. A kind of soft thin cake fried in ghī.

ety: prob. S. तुच्छ+कः; for change of त t to ल l, cf. H. गैल = S. गतः
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Low, mean, paltry, base; bad, wicked, corrupt, vicious, abandoned, depraved, profligate, dissolute, licentious, lewd; — shameless, graceless; addicted to gambling, brawling, rioting, &c.; — a scamp, vagabond, reprobate, a dissolute fellow, a profligate, a libertine, a roué, a rake; (dialec.) a bankrupt

ety: luććā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. (dialec.) = luććāpan, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = luććāpan, q.v.

ety: luććā, q.v.+Prk. प्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Profligacy, libertinism, dissolute life, immorality, depravity, rakishness, debauchery; wantonness, lasciviousness; knavery, roguery, rascality.

ety: luććā, q.v.+Prk. प्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Profligacy, libertinism, dissolute life, immorality, depravity, rakishness, debauchery; wantonness, lasciviousness; knavery, roguery, rascality.
s.m. = luććāpan, q.v.
v.t. = loć denā, q.v.s.v. loć.
s.f. A bad or wanton woman, a depraved (or abandoned) woman, &c. (see luććā).
s.m. = H لچپنا लुचपना lućpanā, s.f. = H لچپنئي लुचपनई lućpanaʼī, s.f. = luććāpan, and luććāpanā, q.v.
s.f. = H لچپن लुचपन lućpan, s.m. = H لچپنئي लुचपनई lućpanaʼī, s.f. = luććāpan, and luććāpanā, q.v.
s.f. = H لچپن लुचपन lućpan, s.m. = H لچپنا लुचपना lućpanā, s.f. = luććāpan, and luććāpanā, q.v.
s.m. A spider.
adj. & s.m. = lutrā, q.v.
s.f. A woman of the Lodhī caste; the wife of a Lodhī, q.v.
s.m. A cloth; a wrapper (cf. lugā, lugṛā).
s.m. lugṛā, q.v.
s.f. A woman, female; wife
subs: To take a wife, to marry
s.m. One who has a wife, a married man.

ety: for A. لغة, v.n. fr. لغو 'to speak'
def: s.f. A word, vocable; speech, language, tongue, dialect, idiom; nomenclature; dictionary, vocabulary, glossary; a form or mode of writing and pronouncing a word
subs: or lug̠at ghaṛnā, To coin big words
s.f. The coining of big words; big words; pedantry
To cull big words out of a dictionary; to use pedantic words.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لُغَت (luġat), from Arabic لُغَة (luḡa). First attested in 1707 as Middle Hindi لغت (lġt /⁠luġat⁠/).

def: s.f. pl. (of lug̠at, q.v.), Tongues, languages, &c.; forms, modes of writing and pronouncing words.
s.m. (rustic) A man, a male (cf. lugāʼī).
s.m. = lugṛā, q.v.
adj. Belonging to language; — verbal, literal
s.f. A mass or lump (of some soft or clammy substance, as dough, kneaded earth, &c.); a ball (of anything that has been pounded or brayed, as levigated medicaments, &c.), a pill, a bolus.
s.m. (f. -ī), Ragged or worn-out cloth; tatter; old clothes; (rustic) garment, wrapper, mantle, sheet (syn. lūgṛā, lokrā, kapṛā).
s.f. (dialec.) = lukṭī or lūkṭī, qq.v.

ety: v.n. fr. لغز 'to change; to distort'
def: s.m. An enigma, a riddle; a puzzle; an ambiguous phrase; anything occult, or mysterious, or difficult to be understood; — a labyrinth.
s.f. A hot wind, &c. ( = luk, lūk, qq.v.).
s.m. Burning, flaming, blazing; flame without smoke; heat (of a fire, &c.).
adj. = lohān, q.v.
adj. = lohān, q.v.
s.m. An iron pot.
s.f. An iron-bound staff or club; a bludgeon; a crowbar.
s.m. A worker in iron, a blacksmith; a smith
subs: 'To sell needles in a blacksmith's shop,' to carry coals to Newcastle
s.f. A blacksmith's forge; a furnace.

def: s.f. A blacksmith's wife.
s.m. = loh-ćūn, q.v.s.v. loh.
adj. & s.m. = lunj, q.v.
s.m. Plenty of water; the great deep; middle of the sea; an abyss
subs: s.f. The agitation (or ebullition) of the abyss.

def: s.f. A fiery phenomenon in the sky; meteor; falling star; firebrand; flame; hot blast (of a furnace, &c.), hot wind (cf. lūk, lū, or lūh).
s.m. Plucking out or off, cutting off; dropping out, elision, rejection.
s.f. Flame; fire: blast (of a furnace); hot wind ( = luk, lū, lūh, lūkā, lūkn).
s.m. Varnish; a kind of glazing composition; — a coating of varnish, &c.

ety: lūk, q.v. + Prk. अओ = S. कः
def: s.m. Flame; fire, &c. ( = lūk, q.v.); a piece of burning matter thrown out from a conflagration, burning grass, &c.
subs: or lūkā lagānā (-meṅ), To put or apply a brand or a torch (to), to set fire (to); — to inflame or excite quarrels (see āg lagānā).

def: adj. & s.m. Vain; roguish; profligate; — a roguish fellow; a scamp; a profligate (syn. luqandrā).
part. adj. (f. -ī), Concealed, hidden, &c.

ety: caus. of luknā; — lukā˚ = luk˚+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To hide, conceal.
s.m. = لقندرا luqandrā, q.v.

ety: said to be corr. fr. qalandar; but cf. luqqā
def: s.m. A scamp, a dissolute fellow, a profligate, a rake, a blackguard; a cheat.
s.m. A kind of ointment, by applying which to a person's eyes he is said to become invisible.
s.m. Hiding, concealment.
s.f. A firebrand, &c. ( = lukāṭhī, q.v.).
s.m. = H لوکٿي लूकटी lūkṭī, s.f. = lukṭī, and lukāṭhī, qq.v.
s.f. Burning wood; a brand, a firebrand; half-burnt wood; a stake burnt at one end; a wooden poker.
v.t. = lukānā, q.v.
